response.text:
Summary:

- Signal type: digital/pulse waveform, not a clean square-wave clock
- Frequency: ~7.86 MHz
- High level: ~3.16 V
- Low level: ~0.39 V average, with excursions down to ~47 mV
- Rise time: ~8.15 ns
- Eye width: ~24.84 ns

What the eye diagram says:
- The eye is open, so the signal is decodable.
- But it is not very clean.
- Transitions are slow and curved.
- The crossing region is spread out, indicating jitter / pattern dependence.
- The low state is inconsistent and sits higher than ideal.

What that means:
- There is usable timing and voltage margin.
- Signal integrity is degraded by bandwidth limits, loading, ringing, or incomplete settling.
- Short pulses do not always settle to the same amplitude.
- High level looks more consistent than low level.

Bottom line:
- This signal will probably work in a tolerant digital receiver,
- but it is not a robust, crisp logic waveform.
- Main concerns are reduced noise margin on the low side and timing uncertainty from smeared crossings.

Best one-line conclusion:
- Open eye, but a noisy/distorted one: functional signal, mediocre signal integrity.
